release_notes: Fixes the GitHub direct-installer uninstaller leaving its autostart .bat in the Windows Startup folder. uninstall_chairside_ready_alert.ps1 only looked for 'Chairside Ready Alert.bat' but the in-app 'Start automatically at login' menu writes 'Start Chairside Ready Alert.bat' (note the leading 'Start '). So if a user enabled autostart from the tray menu and then ran the uninstaller, the .bat survived — and after the install directory was deleted, every subsequent login produced a 'specified path does not exist' popup for the pythonw.exe + script path the .bat captured. The uninstaller now removes both filenames in a single loop. Both the root-level uninstaller (canonical, referenced by version.json) and the bundled copy at 'Chairside Ready Alert Windows Installer/' are updated in lockstep. Microsoft Store builds are unaffected — they use the AppxManifest startupTask extension introduced in 1.0.33 and have no Startup-folder .bat. macOS LaunchAgent uninstall is unaffected.
